name: Color Breathing VR - Relaxation and Mindfulness Meditation through Breathwork

reviews.review_title: The best app for actual meditation
reviews.review_description:
Color breathing VR uses a similar mechanism to Flowborne where you place the controller on your stomach to visualize your breath. While you breathe naturally, colored air flows in and out from you. You get to choose the color based on what emotional state you want to enter versus the one you are starting at. 

This app is very new so there is a lot more to be added content wise but it’s simple design is very effective and has been helping me significantly to center myself through the day.

Overall I really enjoyed this app and found it better than others for actually meditating.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your positive review! You are right that Color Breathing VR was a recently published app and our team is working hard to constantly improve the experience for you all.

reviews.review_description:
Biofeedback is a great choice!

(A mixed reality version would increase the impact.)

developer_response.body: We greatly appreciate your input and look forward to potentially integrating into mixed reality!

reviews.review_description:
I just tried out the app. I have calibrated the right controller and after calibration I see moving white clouds with my breath, so it seems to work. However, when I choose an unguided breathing session and choose some colors, the session does start (I see the rain and hear the music) but the colored clouds are not appearing...
Tried it twice, but no clouds (also calibrated again just in case).
I really like the concept so hope this can be fixed.

Other than that I also have a few suggestions:

 When choosing 'how do you feel now' I miss the option 'neutral'. 
Also it would be nice to be able to choose all the positive feelings there as well. If, for example, I'm already feeling calm/happy/energetic I'm totally fine with just breathing and out that single color (or maybe breathing in white light and breathing out the color to release any residual tension/sadness) in order to have a visual breathing meditation session remaining in a positive state:) 

In general breathing in white light and breathing out dark clouds is powerful, so it could be somerting to just add this as a seperate  option as well.

Love the idea, so looking forward to fixes and future additions.

developer_response.body:
Hello, Life_Is_Energy, we greatly appreciate your feedback and have already began working to make adjustments the experience. 

As for the in experience calibration issues you were running into, I would love to hear more exactly what is going on so that we can resolve this issue. If you would be interested to exchange emails or do a zoom call, feel free to reach out to colorbreathingvr@gmail.com. 

Otherwise, try recalibrating while you're inside the experience as the calibration in the tutorial doesn't carry-over to the in experience calibration.
